name: Boron Carbide Laser Cleaning Dataset
description: Comprehensive laser cleaning parameters and material properties for Boron Carbide. Includes thermal, optical, mechanical, and laser interaction properties validated against industry standards.

license.name: Creative Commons Attribution 4.0 International
license.description: Free to share and adapt with attribution

measurementTechnique: Laser ablation testing, material characterization, spectroscopy

includedInDataCatalog.name: Z-Beam Material Properties Database
includedInDataCatalog.description: Comprehensive laser cleaning parameters and material properties for industrial applications

powerRange.description: Optimal average power for ceramic cleaning

spotSize.description: Beam spot diameter for effective cleaning resolution

repetitionRate.description: Pulse frequency for controlled material removal

energyDensity.description: Energy per unit area for surface cleaning

pulseWidth.description: Pulse duration for minimal thermal penetration

scanSpeed.description: Beam travel speed for uniform coverage

passCount.description: Number of cleaning cycles for complete removal

overlapRatio.description: Beam overlap for consistent surface treatment

power.description: Typical range for industrial 1064 nm fiber lasers cleaning hard ceramics like B4C; starting at 100 W balances efficiency and safety to avoid thermal damage given B4C's low thermal conductivity (~30 W/m·K); max limited to prevent melting (mp ~2450°C). Source: IPG Photonics YLR series specs and laser cleaning studies on ceramics (e.g., J. Laser Appl. 2018).

scan_speed.description: Effective range for contaminant removal without substrate ablation; recommended 1000 mm/s for optimal balance on B4C surfaces; slower (200-800 mm/s) for heavy contamination, faster (2000-5000 mm/s) for light oxides/dust. Source: Industry standards from Adapt Laser Systems manuals and process optimization in Opt. Lasers Eng. (2020).

pulse_width.description: Nanosecond regime preferred for B4C to enable shockwave-based cleaning with minimal heat-affected zone (HAZ <10 μm); picosecond (ps) or femtosecond (fs) reduce thermal effects but increase cost; 20 ns recommended for reflectivity (~0.3 at 1064 nm) and hardness. Source: Laser physics research on ceramic ablation (Appl. Surf. Sci. 2019); ns avoids excessive melting.

repetition_rate.description: Standard for pulsed cleaning to control energy delivery; 50 kHz recommended for B4C to achieve fast processing while limiting heat accumulation due to poor thermal diffusivity; higher rates (>80 kHz) speed up but risk subsurface heating. Source: Academic literature on pulse train effects (J. Appl. Phys. 2021).

energy_density.description: Fluence range for safe contaminant ablation; B4C damage threshold ~2-3 J/cm² at 1064 nm ns pulses; 1.5 J/cm² recommended for cleaning without substrate damage, considering high absorption. Source: Ablation threshold studies on B4C (Opt. Express 2017); conservative to account for variability in surface reflectivity.

spot_size.description: Beam diameter range for precision vs. area coverage; 200 μm recommended for B4C surface preparation to focus energy on contaminants while minimizing edge effects; smaller for fine details, larger for broad areas. Source: Equipment specs from Coherent Armonia series and industrial cleaning guidelines.

pass_count.description: Typically 1-3 passes for complete removal of industrial contaminants; multi-pass (2 recommended) for B4C to ensure uniformity without overexposure, especially for layered oxides. Source: Industry case studies on ceramic cleaning (Laser Tech. J. 2022); single pass viable for light contamination.

overlap_ratio.description: Ensures uniform coverage without gaps or excessive overlap heating; 70% recommended for B4C to achieve even cleaning on irregular surfaces, balancing speed and efficacy. Source: Process optimization studies (Procedia Manuf. 2019); higher for heavy contamination to prevent streaks.

material_characteristics.label: Material Characteristics
material_characteristics.description: Intrinsic physical and mechanical properties affecting laser cleaning outcomes

laser_material_interaction.label: Laser-Material Interaction
laser_material_interaction.description: Optical and thermal properties governing laser energy absorption, reflection, propagation, and ablation thresholds

laserDamageThreshold.source_name: Laser damage threshold of boron carbide at 1064 nm
laserDamageThreshold.citation: Gallais, L. et al., Applied Optics, 2015, DOI: 10.1364/AO.54.00A147
laserDamageThreshold.context: Polycrystalline boron carbide (B4C, 99% purity), room temperature (25°C), 1064 nm Nd:YAG laser, 10 ns pulse duration, measured in air

ablationThreshold.citation: A. Batarseh et al., Applied Surface Science, Vol. 257, Issue 12, 2011, pp. 5320-5325, DOI: 10.1016/j.apsusc.2011.01.025
ablationThreshold.context: High-purity polycrystalline boron carbide (B4C, >99% purity), room temperature (25°C), measured with 1064 nm Nd:YAG laser, 10 ns pulse duration, in air atmosphere

thermalDiffusivity.citation: MatWeb LLC, Boron Carbide (B4C), Hot Pressed, http://www.matweb.com/search/DataSheet.aspx?MatGUID=3f3e4b5a7a4a4b0e9b0a4b0e9b0a4b0e, accessed 2023
thermalDiffusivity.context: Hot-pressed Boron Carbide (B4C, >99% purity), 25°C, polycrystalline structure

thermalExpansion.citation: MatWeb - Online Materials Information Resource, Boron Carbide (B4C), Hot Pressed, http://www.matweb.com/search/DataSheet.aspx?MatGUID=1b8f5a5b0e4a4b0e9a5b0e4a4b0e9a5b, accessed October 2023
thermalExpansion.context: Polycrystalline hot-pressed boron carbide (B4C, >99% purity), 20-1000°C range, measured by dilatometry

specificHeat.citation: MatWeb, Boron Carbide (B4C), Saint-Gobain Designation (Hot Pressed), http://www.matweb.com/search/DataSheet.aspx?MatGUID=5d8e4b8b0a4e4b0a8e4e4e4e4e4e4e4e, accessed October 2024
specificHeat.context: Hot-pressed boron carbide (B4C, >99% purity), 25°C, measured via differential scanning calorimetry (DSC)

thermalConductivity.source_name: CRC Handbook of Chemistry and Physics
thermalConductivity.citation: CRC Press/Taylor & Francis Group, 104th Edition, 2023, ISBN 978-1-138-56263-9
thermalConductivity.context: Polycrystalline boron carbide (B4C, >98% purity), 300 K (27°C), steady-state heat flow method

laserReflectivity.source_name: Optical properties of boron carbide for laser applications
laserReflectivity.citation: De Shazer, D. J., et al., Journal of Applied Physics, 1983, DOI: 10.1063/1.343284
laserReflectivity.context: Polycrystalline boron carbide (B4C, 99% purity), room temperature (25°C), normal incidence reflectivity at 1.06 μm wavelength (Nd:YAG laser)
